expectations.text: Output includes standard .kql comment header block
expectations.evidence: File has a two-line comment header ('// Lateral Movement Detection — ...', '// Detects accounts...', '// Tables: DeviceLogonEvents...') but NOT a structured block with labeled fields for Title, Service, Tables, Description, Parameters, and Complexity in the skill's standard format.

expectations.text: Groups by 1-hour time bins (bin(Timestamp, 1h) or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: Uses sliding-window join: 'where Timestamp2 >= Timestamp and Timestamp2 < Timestamp + RollingWindow' where RollingWindow = 1h. Equivalent to grouping by 1h windows.

expectations.text: Counts distinct devices/machines per account per time window
expectations.evidence: 'UniqueDevices = dcount(DeviceName2)' in the summarize, grouped by AccountId and WindowStart = Timestamp

expectations.text: Filters for accounts hitting more than 5 distinct devices
Covers the last 48 hours as specified
expectations.evidence: 'let LookbackWindow = 48h;' and '| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackWindow)' applied to both sides of the join

claims.claim: Uses AccountId = tolower(strcat(AccountDomain, '\\', AccountName)) as composite identity
claims.evidence: 'extend AccountId = tolower(strcat(AccountDomain, "\\\\", AccountName))' normalizes the identity before joining

eval_feedback.overall: Header assertion correctly differentiates skill from no-skill output. Other logic is functionally equivalent.
